100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 8, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva, addressing global disarmament amid rising tensions in Europe. In the United States, a significant labor strike in the coal industry drew national attention, highlighting ongoing disputes between miners and mine operators. Meanwhile, the construction of the iconic Chrysler Building in New York City continued to rise, promising to be the tallest skyscraper in the world upon completion.
Updated: Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 07:46:05 AM CDT
